appliedUpdates.description: Added Amp provider alias routing architecture detail to architecture.md: documents that /api/provider/:provider/ routes are a separate route group (ampProviders) that also needs ACL middleware to avoid bypass paths.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: prefer sync.Once-based helpers for gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode) in tests rather than init() functions. The init() pattern causes data races under -race when multiple test packages are compiled together.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: internal/api/model_acl_test.go uses init() for gin.SetMode, while internal/api/handlers/management/gin_test_mode_test.go uses a sync.Once helper. The init() pattern caused race conditions that required a follow-up commit (57d53acc) to fix in management tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance that post-implementation review may require extending ACL/security middleware wiring to adjacent shipped route modules beyond the upstream PR file list. Workers should audit all route groups that serve model-bearing endpoints, not just the ones touched by the upstream PR.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker session 9c393af7 had to extend ACL middleware to Amp provider alias routes (amp.go, routes.go, modules.go) that were not in the upstream PR #2914 file list but were necessary because the Amp provider aliases served model-bearing routes that bypassed the new ACL middleware.

rejectedObservations.observation: environment.md was correctly updated to record that the race gate is actionable with the compiler path and CGO setup.
services.yaml race_pr2914 command correctly documents the Windows-specific compiler path and CGO setup.
